answer:
The correct answer is **E. (ii) and (iii) only**.

An \( R^2 = 0 \) means the regression line perfectly fails to explain any variation in \( y \), so the fitted line is horizontal (ii) and explains none of the variability (iii). However, the slope coefficients (i) and intercept (iv) need not be zero—only their collective explanatory power is zero.
